culling.blurThresholdDesc: Images with a sharpness score below this value are flagged. Higher is stricter.

culling.rankFocusDesc: Sort the batch by center-weighted sharpness and confidence without selecting images for rejection.
culling.similarityThresholdDesc: Lower is stricter (exact duplicates). Higher is looser (near duplicates). A value of 24-32 is recommended.

negativeConversion.acquisitionHealthHint: Estimated from filename extension and path tokens only; pixel quality is not inspected.
negativeConversion.acquisitionHealthLimit: Not checked: clipping, bit depth, color profiles, lab processing, or final inversion quality.
negativeConversion.autoBaseFogTooltip: Estimate film base and balance color weights
negativeConversion.baseSamplingCtaAcceptHint: Export is blocked until the measured base sample is accepted.
negativeConversion.baseSamplingCtaEstimateHint: Export is blocked because the negative base has not been sampled yet. Estimate the base from the active scan or sample a visible film border.
negativeConversion.baseSamplingCtaReadyHint: Base sampling is accepted and the positive preview can be saved when the remaining workflow gates are ready.
negativeConversion.baseSamplingCtaTitle: Sample the film base before saving
negativeConversion.conversionBundleHint: Write a JSON bundle with replay metadata, source refs, output hashes, sidecars, and profile provenance.
negativeConversion.customBaseSampleHint: Measure a normalized base/fog rectangle, then explicitly apply it to conversion.

dustCandidateFilter.empty: No dust candidates match this filter.

dustHealRuntimeProofStatus.needs_accepted_corrections: Accept dust candidates to create editable heal layers.
dustHealRuntimeProofStatus.needs_real_raw_output_proof: Partial proof: validate preview/export pixels on real RAWs before closing.

negativeConversion.dustScratchReviewHint: Workspace-level inspection state for preview/export handoff; pixel retouch remains a follow-up.
negativeConversion.effectiveFrameRgbBalance: Effective frame RGB R {{red}} / G {{green}} / B {{blue}}
negativeConversion.highlightRecoveryApplyWarning: Highlight recovery is outside the safe apply gate.
negativeConversion.neutralPatchApplyWarning: Sample correction is outside the safe apply gate.
negativeConversion.noticeText: Inversion logic inspired by <0>NegPy</0> created by marcinz606 (<1>GPL-3.0</1>).
negativeConversion.patchSamplerHint: Probe shadows, highlights, and base strips without changing the active conversion.
negativeConversion.positiveHandoffOpenInEditor: Open first saved positive in editor
negativeConversion.profileMeasuredClaimPolicy: Process-family measured profile; no named-stock or colorimetric-match claim.
negativeConversion.qcProofHint: Contact-sheet proof state for batch handoff; image pixels are not retouched here.
negativeConversion.scanInputGuidanceAvoidPositive: Use the original negative capture or scan, not a pre-inverted positive.
negativeConversion.scanInputGuidanceAvoidProofs: Avoid lab proofs, contact sheets, and edited JPEG exports as conversion sources.
negativeConversion.scanInputGuidanceDisableAuto: Disable scanner or lab automatic exposure, color, contrast, sharpening, and inversion corrections.
negativeConversion.scanInputGuidanceLimit: File-type warnings are inferred from the path and extension only; they do not inspect pixels, detect clipping or prior processing, or predict inversion quality.
negativeConversion.scanInputGuidancePreferred: Prefer camera RAW, DNG, or 16-bit TIFF scans with consistent exposure.
negativeConversion.scanInputGuidancePreserveBorders: Preserve film borders or base fog areas so sampling can estimate the negative base.
negativeConversion.shadowBlackPointApplyWarning: Shadow endpoint correction is outside the safe apply gate.
negativeConversion.stockMetadataPolicyDetail: Named stocks are references only until project-owned measurements prove a runtime profile.
negativeConversion.stockReferenceCoverageEmpty: No named stock references currently map to this profile.
negativeConversion.stockReferenceCoverageSummary: {{referenceCount}} named stock reference(s) map to this generic runtime preset.
negativeConversion.title: Negative Conversion
negativeConversion.walkthroughClosureHint: One place to verify the import, profile, inversion, QC, handoff, and export path.
negativeConversion.walkthroughClosureProofBoundary: Proof boundary: UI/runtime state only until full manual app verification on real RAW/scan output is captured.

controls.zoomDesc: Adjust how fast the canvas zooms in and out when using the scroll wheel or pinch gesture.

data.clearSidecarsDesc: This will delete all .rrdata files (containing your edits) within your root folders:
data.clearThumbnailDesc: This will delete all cached thumbnail images. They will be regenerated automatically as you browse your library.
data.logsDesc: View the application's log file for troubleshooting. The log is located at:

modals.aiMessage:
Are you sure you want to remove all AI-generated tags from all images in all active root folders?

This will not affect user-added tags. This action cannot be undone.
modals.allMessage:
Are you sure you want to remove all AI-generated and user-added tags from all images in all active root folders?

This action cannot be undone.
modals.cacheMessage:
Are you sure you want to clear the thumbnail cache?

All thumbnails will need to be regenerated, which may be slow for large folders.
modals.sidecarMessage:
Are you sure you want to delete all sidecar files?

This will permanently remove all your edits for all images inside all active root folders and their subfolders.

statuses.allSuccess: {{count}} files updated. All non-color tags removed.
statuses.allSuccess_one: {{count}} file updated. All non-color tags removed.
statuses.allSuccess_other: {{count}} files updated. All non-color tags removed.
statuses.clearingAi: Clearing AI tags from all sidecar files...
statuses.clearingAll: Clearing all tags from sidecar files...
statuses.failedToGetPath: Could not retrieve log file path.

data.title: Data Management

general.createXmpDesc: Automatically create a new XMP sidecar file if one does not exist for an image.
general.displayEditIconDesc: Show a small slider icon on thumbnails that have been edited.
general.focusModeDesc: Helps you focus by automatically closing other panels when you open a new one.
general.folderImageCountsDesc: Show the number of images inside folders when hovering over the folder tree.
general.nativeTitlebarDesc: Use your system's default window titlebar instead of RapidRAW's custom one.
general.themeDesc: Change the look and feel of the application.
general.title: General Settings
general.xmpSyncDesc: Sync ratings, color labels and tags to standard XMP sidecar files for compatibility with other photo editors.

lenses.description: Create a list of your frequently used lenses to quickly access them in the Lens Correction panel.
lenses.title: My Lenses

cloud.description: For those who want a simpler solution, an optional subscription provides the same high-quality results as self-hosting without any hassle. This is the most convenient option and the best way to support the project.

signedOut.upgradeDesc: To use Cloud AI features, you need a Cloud subscription.

cloud.title: Cloud Service

connector.addressDesc: Enter the address and port of your running AI Connector instance. Required for generative AI features.
connector.description: For users with a capable GPU who want maximum control, connect RapidRAW to your own Connector server. This gives you full control for technical workflows.
connector.title: Self-Hosted (RapidRAW AI Connector)

cpu.description: Integrated directly into RapidRAW, these features run entirely on your computer. They are fast, free, and require no setup, making them ideal for everyday workflow acceleration.
cpu.title: Built-in AI (CPU)

ai.description: RapidRAW's AI is built for flexibility. Choose your ideal workflow, from fast local tools to powerful self-hosting.
ai.title: Generative AI

processing.backendDesc: Select the graphics API. 'Auto' is recommended. May fix crashes on some systems.
processing.dynamicDesc: The editor renders the preview to match your display's actual pixel density. This ensures that every detail is represented with 1:1 pixel accuracy, providing maximum clarity when zooming and checking focus.
processing.highDpiDesc: Render previews at your screen's native {{dpr}}x physical pixel resolution. Produces the sharpest possible preview but uses significantly more memory.
processing.highDpiDescStandard: This setting only affects high-DPI displays. Your current display is standard resolution.
processing.imageCacheDesc: Maximum number of full-resolution images kept in RAM. Higher values make switching between recently edited images instant, but use significantly more memory.
processing.linuxCompatDesc: Enable workarounds for common GPU driver and display server issues. Disable this to enable full GPU acceleration.
processing.livePreviewQualityDesc: Controls the resolution and compression of the image while dragging sliders. Lower quality significantly improves responsiveness.
processing.livePreviewsDesc: Update the preview immediately while dragging sliders. Disable this if the interface feels laggy during adjustments.

preprocessing.applyPreprocessingDesc: If enabled, the base color noise reduction and pre-sharpening above will also be applied to standard formats.
preprocessing.captureSharpeningPresetDesc: Applies a schema-backed starting point to the RAW preprocessing sharpening, chroma noise reduction, and non-RAW opt-in controls.
preprocessing.colorNrDesc: Applies a cross-bilateral filter early in the pipeline to remove chroma noise. Higher value = Stronger NR.
preprocessing.defaultNonRawTonemapperDesc: The tonemapper to apply to non-RAW images (e.g., JPEG, PNG).
preprocessing.defaultRawTonemapperDesc: The tonemapper to apply to RAW images.
preprocessing.highlightRecoveryDesc: Controls how much detail is recovered from clipped highlights in RAW files. Higher values recover more detail but can introduce purple artefacts.
preprocessing.linearRawDesc: Fixes color casts or pink tint in some DNG files. Controls how already processed LinearRAW data is interpreted.
preprocessing.sharpeningDesc: Applies gentle detail enhancement early in the pipeline. Higher value = More sharpening.
preprocessing.sharpeningDetailDesc: Controls how strongly fine detail contributes to the early RAW sharpening pass.
preprocessing.sharpeningEdgeMaskingDesc: Protects low-contrast tones from sharpening so noise and flat areas stay cleaner.
preprocessing.sharpeningRadiusDesc: Sets the blur radius used to extract capture detail before global edits.
preprocessing.title: Image Preprocessing
preprocessing.tonemapperOverrideDesc: Force a specific tonemapper globally for all images, hiding the tonemapper switch from the adjustments panel.

processing.previewResDesc: Determines the maximum resolution of the preview. Lower values significantly improve performance.

rawMode.description: Applies a versioned RAW development recipe so speed, default quality, and maximum detail are understandable and repeatable.
rawMode.label: RAW Processing Mode
rawMode.title: RAW Quality Mode

balanced.description: Default-quality demosaic, highlight recovery, chroma cleanup, and gentle capture sharpening.
balanced.label: Balanced

fast.description: Speed demosaic with capture preprocessing disabled for responsive culling and quick edits.
fast.label: Fast

maximum.description: Full-quality demosaic with stronger highlight headroom, chroma cleanup, and detail-safe capture sharpening.
maximum.label: Maximum

processing.renderScaleDesc: Scales the render resolution relative to your display. Lower values improve performance on high-resolution screens at the cost of some sharpness.
processing.restartRequired: Changes to the processing engine require an application restart to take effect.
processing.staticDesc: The editor renders the image at a fixed resolution. This mode is the fastest and most consistent, making it ideal for lower-end hardware where smooth performance is prioritized over pixel-perfect zoom.
processing.staticPreviewResDesc: Sets the resolution for static previews like crop mode, lens correction, and perspective tools. Does not affect the main editor preview.
processing.thumbnailResDesc: Determines the resolution of generated library thumbnails. Higher values produce sharper images during loading.
processing.title: Processing Engine
processing.wgpuDescAndroid: Bypasses browser encoding for instantly responsive live previews. (Disabled on Android: Native WGPU surface creation is currently not supported alongside the mobile webview.)
processing.wgpuDescLinux: Bypasses browser encoding for instantly responsive live previews. (Disabled on Linux: Tauri uses GTK for webviews, which conflicts with WGPU on the same X11 surface and causes flickering.)
processing.wgpuDescRecommended: Bypasses browser encoding for instantly responsive live previews. Highly recommended for performance.
processing.workerThreadsDesc: Number of parallel threads used to generate thumbnails. Higher values speed up library loading but use more CPU & RAM.

tagging.aiTaggingDesc: Enables automatic image tagging using an AI (CLIP) model. This will download an additional model and impact performance while browsing folders. Tags are used for searching a folder.
tagging.clearAiTagsDesc: This will remove all AI-generated tags from your .rrdata files in all active root folders. User-added tags will be kept.
tagging.clearAllTagsDesc: This will remove all AI-generated and user-added tags from your .rrdata files in all active root folders. Color labels will be kept.
tagging.customListDesc: If provided, the AI will ONLY use tags from this list, overriding RapidRAW’s built-in list. Tagging works only in English.
tagging.maxAiTagsDesc: The maximum number of tags to generate per image.
tagging.noCustomTags: No custom AI tags (Using built-in list)
tagging.shortcutsDesc: A list of tags that will appear as shortcuts in the tagging context menu.
tagging.title: Tagging

thanks.description: A huge thank you to the following projects that were very important in the development of RapidRAW:

list.darktable: For some reference implementations that guided parts of this work.
list.depth: For the powerful monocular depth estimation model that enables the AI depth masking capabilities.
list.lama: For the powerful & simple image inpainting model, which enables content-aware fill and object removal.
list.lensfun: For its invaluable open-source library and comprehensive database for automatic lens correction.
list.negpy: For the inspiration behind the negative conversion logic.
list.nind: For providing AI models that power the AI noise reduction capabilities in RapidRAW.
list.rawler: For the excellent Rust crate that provides the foundation for RAW file processing in this project.
list.sam2: For providing the foundation model used for the AI subject detection capabilities.
list.u2net: For providing the robust architecture used for the AI sky and foreground detection capabilities.
list.you: For using and supporting RapidRAW. Your interest keeps this project alive and evolving.
